note:
文学诞生的那一天，并不是一个男孩喊狼来了，狼从尼安德特山谷跑出来，后面跟着一只大灰狼：文学诞生在一个男孩喊狼来的日子，狼来了，他身后没有狼。这个可怜的小家伙因为经常撒谎，最终被一只真正的野兽吃掉了，这是相当偶然的。但这里有一点很重要。在高草里的狼和高楼里的狼之间，有一个闪闪发光的中间人。那个中间人，那个棱镜，就是文学艺术。
让我给你讲一个关于一个男孩的故事。他于1914年5月22日出生于阿拉巴马州伯明翰。他的母亲艾达·布朗特是一名女服务员。她最喜欢的表演者是一位名叫黑赫尔曼的杂耍舞台魔术师，他会表演各种各样的戏法：悬浮、变兔子魔术、逃跑。他表演的亮点是一个可怕的亵渎神明的奇迹：他将被活埋在“黑赫尔曼的私人墓地”，然后在三天后被挖掘出来，凯旋重返舞台。艾达非常钦佩黑赫尔曼，所以她用他的名字给她的儿子取名。
年轻的赫尔曼·普尔·布朗特(Herman Poole Blount)成为音乐神童也就不足为奇了，因为有了这样一位大胆、变幻莫测的同名表演者。十二岁时，他就能看懂钢琴曲并自己作曲了。十几岁时，他能凭记忆重现由艾灵顿公爵(Duke Ellington)和胖子沃勒(Fats Waller)等伟人领导的穿过伯明翰的大型乐队音乐会。在种族隔离的工业高中就读期间，赫尔曼加入了几个爵士乐和R&Amp；B乐队，其中一个乐队是由他的生物老师埃塞尔·哈珀(Ethel Harper)领导的。哈珀离开乐队后，赫尔曼接管了乐队，并在剩下的巡演中用他的昵称-桑尼·布朗特管弦乐团(Sonny Blount Orchestra)重新命名了乐队。桑尼是一名优等生，1936年获得奖学金，在阿拉巴马州黑人农业和机械学院学习音乐教育。
这些太空人联系了我。他们想让我和他们一起去外太空…。。我必须在我身体的任何部位都不接触光束…的情况下上去。。它看起来就像一个巨大的聚光灯照在我身上，我称之为跨分子化，我的整个身体变成了其他东西。我可以看穿自己。我就上去了。现在，我称之为能量转换，因为我不是人类形态。我以为我在那里，但我能看穿自己。
然后我降落在一颗我认为是土星…的行星上。。他们传送了我，我和他们一起在台上。他们想和我谈谈。他们的每只耳朵上都有一个小天线。每只眼睛上方都有一个小天线。他们跟我谈过了。他们告诉我停止(上大学)，因为在学校里会有很大的麻烦。…。。世界正在进入一片混乱…。。我会(通过音乐)说话，全世界都会倾听。
在这次有远见的经历之后，桑尼从大学退学了。他开始痴迷于太空旅行和各种深奥的东西。他把他在阿拉巴马州的家的一楼改造成了一个沙龙，在那里他可以和经过的当地音乐家和漂流者一起写、转录、录制和排练歌曲。一个集体，一个星系--桑尼在中心，其他人围着他转--非常适合他。
28岁时，赫尔曼·布朗特应征参加第二次世界大战。他宣布自己是出于医学和道德原因而依良心拒服兵役的人。当他没有出现在分配给他的候补服务时，他被逮捕了。在法庭上，布朗特为自己辩护，就法律和圣经解释问题与法官进行辩论。当法官说他无论如何都应该被征召入伍时，布朗特说他会杀死他遇到的第一位高级军官。法官将他关押在沃克县监狱，直到他的案件得到进一步处理。“我从来没有见过像你这样的黑鬼，”法官说。“不，”布朗特回答说，“你再也不会了。”
在三十九年后“不快乐和困惑的…”在监狱里“几乎疯狂”的日子里，桑尼上诉被送到一个平民公共服务营地。他最终来到了宾夕法尼亚州，在那里他白天练习林业，晚上弹钢琴。那里的精神病学家将他描述为“精神变态人格”，易患神经性抑郁症和性变态，是“一个受过良好教育的有色人种知识分子”。大约一个月后，他被认定为不适合服兵役(他的疝气被评为“4级”)，并被送回伯明翰。
两年后，桑尼前往芝加哥重新开始他的音乐生涯。他畅饮了“风之城”，畅饮了充满活力的爵士乐和歌舞表演场面，装饰艺术风格的建筑和纪念碑，以及错综复杂的黑人民族主义历史。从一个乐队跳到另一个乐队，他终于组建了自己的乐队。他称它为阿克斯特拉。1952年10月20日，桑尼模仿了埃及神话，再次给自己改名。他后来成为Le Sony‘r Ra，或简称为Sun Ra。他说，赫尔曼·布朗特是一个奴隶名字。
Sun Ra的照片经常让人联想到混乱的混合：祭司般的未来主义服装和布景，古埃及和土星形成了过去和未来乌托邦的复印件。他的声音综合了大乐队、摇摆乐、硬波普、雷鬼、非洲流行音乐、电子音乐和华特迪士尼音乐剧。他的引用--表达在他的歌词、诗歌和小册子中--也展示了这种折衷主义：卡巴拉、灵知主义、共济会、泛非洲主义、禅宗。1971年，当他在加州大学伯克利分校(University of California，Berkeley)教授一门课程时，他的教学大纲包括埃及死亡之书；19世纪俄罗斯媒体布拉瓦茨基夫人(Madame Blavatsky)的神学著作；1968年被纽约市交通警察枪杀的杰出诗人亨利·大仲马(Henry Dumas)。他经常引用乔治·G·M·詹姆斯(George G.M.James)的“被盗遗产”(1954)，该书声称希腊哲学从埃及神话中窃取了思想。
在孙拉的各种著作和采访中，他总是坚持他所谓的“方程式”有一个形而上学的基础：无序链的形而上学和符号学，命理学和词源学。他有一点古鲁对个人和集体的反响。孙若常聚集弟子，却又与他们格格不入。他的传记作者约翰·F·斯威德引用孙拉的话说：“我知道他们在说什么，但他们不知道我在说什么。我正在做他们正在做的事情，但他们从来没有在我脑海中留下深刻印象。“。他是独狼和狼人领袖，阿努比斯掌管着无边无际的黑色世界、地下世界和外太空。
1974年，孙拉·科罗特制作并主演了一部电影，这部电影通常被认为是我们现在所说的非未来主义的起源文本。太空的前提是让我们回到他的星际愿景的地方：孙拉想要“为黑人建立一个殖民地”…。。我们将通过同位素隐形传态，跨分子传送，或者更好的方式，通过音乐将整个星球传送到这里。“。
他一度在加利福尼亚州奥克兰成立了外层空间就业局，在那里他为这次旅行采访了湾区居民。孙拉坐在柜台后面，戴着银色的头盔，穿着点缀着闪闪发光的星星的长袍。一个中年黑人男子走了过来，他穿着一件三角衫，尖领衬衫外面穿着格子夹克，戴着一条珠子和骨头组成的项链：
醉汉：这是什么？我是说，我-就像，呃，我在哪里？你知道，呃，你是谁？
酒鬼：没什么？！嗯，你最好告诉我什么都不是，因为，嗯(咯咯笑)，我需要一份工作。我-我不知道该怎么办。
酒鬼：什么都没有？！什么都没有？！但我得喝点东西，这样我就能再拿一瓶了。我不能去那狗屎，…
申请者的俚语-“什-像，呃，我在哪里？你知道，呃，你是谁？“--孙若禅--”我什么都不是“--构成了一种令人眼花缭乱的、对位的黑色形而上学。26年前，拉尔夫·埃里森(Ralph Ellison)在他的散文“哈莱姆无处可去”(Harlem Is Nowhere)中发现，黑人说着同样丰富的比喻语言：“在哈莱姆，回答问候的是‘你好吗？’。”经常是，‘哦，天哪，I’m Nowhere…‘“。
美国黑人正不顾一切地寻找一种身份。拒绝给予他们的二等地位，他们感觉被疏远了，他们的整个生活变成了对以下问题的探索：我是谁，我是什么，我为什么是我，我在哪里？
到目前为止，这是一种熟悉的黑人身份否定神学。它也像一个真空，一个回旋，处于非未来主义的核心。当学者马克·德里(Mark Dery)在1992年首次创造这个词时，他把它放在缺席的情况下，对似乎缺乏非洲未来主义传统感到惊讶：
为什么很少有非洲裔美国人写科幻小说，这种类型的科幻小说与另一个人--陌生土地上的陌生人--的亲密接触似乎是唯一适合非裔美国小说家关注的问题的体裁？…。非洲未来主义的概念引发了一个令人不安的矛盾：一个社区的过去被故意抹去，其精力随后被消耗在寻找清晰的历史痕迹上，难道一个社区能想象可能的未来吗？
但黑人传统中的这种虚无主义也有一个反作用：没有什么可以成为一切，没有任何地方可以成为其他地方。弗朗茨·法农穿着黑皮肤，戴着白色面具，
只要我想要…。我不仅仅是此时此地，被封存于万物之中。我是为了别的地方，为了别的东西。我要求注意我的否定行为，只要我追求生命以外的东西，只要我为创造一个人类世界而战。
在“太空就是这个地方”中，孙拉招募了黑人人类来陪伴他去其他地方，也就是土星，通过精确地呼吁这个共享的无存在之地。“我不是真的，”他对一群持怀疑态度的年轻男女说：
我和你一样。你不存在于这个社会里。如果你这样做了，你的人民就不会寻求平等的权利。你不是真的。如果你是这样的话，你在世界各国中就会有一定的地位了。所以，我们都是神话。我来找你不是为了现实。我是以神话的身份来找你的。因为那就是黑人，神话。
黑色物质本身-外层空间，以其所有天鹅绒般的丰富的零-成为黑色能源的来源，黑色的未来。一方面，孙拉说，“黑暗。没人能做到这一点。它就这么发生了。光和所有这些东西-有人做的；上面写着是他们做的。但是没有人创造了黑暗。我的音乐是关于黑暗传统的。黑暗传统的意义远不止黑人传统。“。另一方面，太阳拉以太阳本身的名字命名自己，那滚滚的光之源，从宇宙的黑暗中闪耀、飞溅和飞奔而出。
他们不知从哪里冒出来，就像余烬突然燃烧起来，活生生的伸展到他的手中，他的螺旋形无限，他的存在。
美丽的金色巢，柔软而闪闪发光，颜色繁多，…。像太阳一样容光焕发。是的，我有一个巢；在世界的顶端的外层空间。
他用双重否定变戏法，比如“从来不是”，然后强调“这个”这样的指示语：
他乘以否定--“双重否定”--并将其翻转为“开”：
On-ness Reach要求它是…。光线将它们包裹起来，就像一张由亲切的棱镜组成的网状…。他们会的，他们会挺身而出，走向直觉的两面性。
他的爵士乐也疯狂振荡，从混乱转向旋律，将不和谐转化为和声，在闪闪发光的音符和突如其来的寂静之间飘荡-晕厥使节奏成为节奏。孙拉的各种形式的艺术都向黑人提出了这样的挑战：如果我们什么都不是，如果我们只是神话，为什么不把它变成文字，为什么不让它成为物质呢？为什么不创造，为什么不成为闪闪发光的黑物质呢？
摄影师明·史密斯1978年拍摄的Sun Ra照片完美地捕捉到了这种能量，这种哲学。*史密斯在70年代作为一名模特开始了她的职业生涯，这让她接触到了那个时代许多重要的黑人文化人物，包括阿尔文·艾利(Alvin Ailey)、詹姆斯·鲍德温(James Baldwin)、格雷斯·琼斯(Grace Jones)、妮娜·西蒙妮(Nina Simone)和孙拉(Sun Ra)。1973年，史密斯成为第一位加入位于哈莱姆区的摄影团体Kamoinger的女性；她也是第一位作品被Mo MA收购的黑人女性摄影师。史密斯的摄影以其精湛的模糊、音乐作家格雷格·泰特(Greg Tate)所称的“视觉颤动”的振动品质而闻名，以平整前景和背景，但又通过精确的纹理并置来描绘它们而闻名。她有时在她的照片上画上漩涡和斑点，使黑色中的棱镜颜色浮出水面，这是托因·奥吉·奥杜托拉绘画的一种先驱。
我们可以称之为史密斯的华丽的、解构的明暗对比也源于她在照明、快门速度以及相机和被摄体的相对运动方面的技术创新-所有这些都源于沉浸，即坚持在黑色空间拍摄黑人。正如艺术家亚瑟·雅法(Arthur Jafa)在一次对话中所说的那样，这篇文章将与这篇关于史密斯作品的Aperture卷一起发表，“事实上，她正在做的事情的技术参数是由致力于置身于黑人占据的空间而构建的。”被照亮的…的空间。秘密空间。“。爵士乐俱乐部的环境放大了这种隐秘空间、身体碰撞和高对比度的美学。史密斯曾与爵士音乐家大卫·默里结婚一段时间，并陪同他巡演；他们的儿子明格斯也是一名音乐家。
在史密斯的许多照片中，黑人音乐家在灯光下用铜管乐器，明暗对比度被抽象和强化。这不是黑白，而是黑暗和光亮；我们不仅仅是混合、流血和模糊，而是移动、颤抖和颤抖。在黑暗中照亮口吃的度数，留下棱柱状的痕迹，火焰般的涂鸦和翘曲。其结果不是缺席和在场之间的滑稽表演，而是更多的节奏-它们的切分散布。这就是明·史密斯如何唤起孙拉明亮黑色的形而上学的精确性、诡异和欢乐。
在她的照片“太阳拉空间II”中，光不仅勾勒出他的轮廓，还构成了他。Sun Ra是聚光灯，是变形的微光，也是一座纪念碑。他的西装已经变成了镶嵌的石头，上面装饰着披肩、头饰和太阳镜的闪耀光芒-这些看起来像是字面上的，是由太阳制成的眼镜。他们的形状与上方的电灯泡相呼应，并由右侧的玩家倒置，玩家戴着光线模糊的墨镜。倒影将第三只眼睛投射到孙拉的额头上，明亮而扭曲--摇摇晃晃的天线或他的Arkestra成员正在演奏的铜管乐器的琴键。他们是他身后苍白杂乱的线条和形状，他们的圆帽与左边墙上的黑眼圈形成了对比-又像一件乐器，就像一个巨大的长笛的钥匙孔。灯光是金属的，阴影是铰接的--摇晃着，发出刺耳的声音。场景是歪斜的，背后是中秋瞥见的天花板网格，提醒我们史密斯就在那里，正在移动。孙拉也在移动，他为我们行凶抢劫--半清醒的，极其庄严的--当他转身时，他的斗篷就像银河系张开的手臂。但不知何故，他也是完美静止的，风暴之眼，螺旋的中心。
在孙罗空间I中，他面朝外，纯净的表面。我们看到他披风的翼展，他的后脑勺有纹理：一边是划伤的地板，另一边是机器般的蜂窝，上方是一个白色的长方形，窗户是深色的--就像火车或立面一样。在照片的中央，孙拉的头饰粗糙明亮的圆球上，他的背部上下放射着光芒，像星星一样散落着，从褶皱的沙丘上滚落下来。光明和黑暗是雾化的，漂浮的：埃及的月光下的沙子，一个昏暗的星座。放在一起看，这两张照片暗示着格式塔的转变，孙拉在中转，孙拉转身离开，纯粹的飞行，纯粹的能量-透明本身：“我可以看透自己。我就上去了。“。因此，史密斯的二元体传达了孙拉机智的轻盈，他的奇迹：魔术师旋转然后消失，散落到空中。
Jafa观察到，在史密斯的许多照片中，“你不能识别任何人”，因为她接受了“在某种意义上使照片作为证据的能力无效的技术”。毫无疑问，这就是孙拉，不过，他身上散发着所有宝石般的光环，从他的电影镜头中散发出光环--“石头会说话…”通过美的振动，“孙拉在1980年的纪录片”孙拉：欢乐的噪音“中提到埃及象形文字。在这里，史密斯消失的品质并没有掩饰或抹去他，而是隐藏在他显眼的存在之中。我们在旋转、搭接层、颗粒状的光泽、星座的景观和光谱社区中感受到了它。孙拉在那里又不在那里，就像史密斯在那里又不在那里一样：“我什么都有，什么都不是。”这两位艺术家没有将对黑色的否定归因于前后不一致或悲观，而是敢于让我们从无到有地重新想象黑人身份-也就是人类身份-作为一种存在秩序，它在虚无中断断续续，然后及时溶解和聚集自己和他人。
孙拉真的相信他曾经被跨分子化为土星吗？他真的想通过把黑人送上太空来拯救他们吗？他是不是某种星际马库斯·加维(Marcus Garvey)，卖回非洲的机票，但从未启航？他是不是在搞一个精心策划的恶作剧？有关系吗？孙若从来没有打破性格，从来没有说过“这一切都是个玩笑”，“这只是一个骗局”，或者“这个世界让我发疯”。由于战略上的疏忽，他把一个“黑鬼”变成了“有色人种”。
note_en:
Literature was born not the day when a boy crying wolf, wolf came running out of the Neanderthal valley with a big gray wolf at his heels: literature was born on the day when a boy came crying wolf, wolf and there was no wolf behind him. That the poor little fellow because he lied too often was finally eaten up by a real beast is quite incidental. But here is what is important. Between the wolf in the tall grass and the wolf in the tall story there is a shimmering go-between. That go-between, that prism, is the art of literature.
  Let me tell you a story about a boy. He was born on May 22, 1914, in Birmingham, Alabama. His mother, Ida Blount, was a waitress. Her favorite performer was a vaudeville stage magician named Black Herman, who did all manner of tricks: levitation, rabbit conjure, escape. The highlight of his act was a ghastly, blasphemous miracle: he would get buried alive in “Black Herman’s Private Graveyard,” then be exhumed three days later to make a triumphant return to the stage. Ida admired Black Herman so much that she named her son after him.
 With such a bold, phantasmagoric performer for a namesake, it’s perhaps no surprise that young Herman Poole Blount became a musical prodigy. By age twelve, he was sight-reading piano music and composing his own. As a teenager, he could reproduce from memory the big-band concerts that came through Birmingham, led by greats like Duke Ellington and Fats Waller. While attending the segregated Industrial High School, Herman joined a handful of jazz and  R&B bands, including one led by his biology teacher, Ethel Harper. When Harper left the group, Herman took it over and renamed it with his nickname—the Sonny Blount Orchestra—for the rest of the tour. An honor-roll student, Sonny got a scholarship in 1936 to study music education at the Alabama State Agricultural and Mechanical Institute for Negroes.
  These space men contacted me. They wanted me to go to outer space with them…. I’d have to go up with no part of my body touching outside of the beam…. It looked like a giant spotlight shining down on me, and I call it transmolecularization, my whole body changed into something else. I could see through myself. And I went up. Now, I call that an energy transformation because I wasn’t in human form. I thought I was there, but I could see through myself.
 Then I landed on a planet that I identified as Saturn…. They teleported me, and I was down there on that stage with them. They wanted to talk with me. They had one little antenna on each ear. A little antenna over each eye. They talked to me. They told me to stop [attending college] because there was going to be great trouble in schools…. The world was going into complete chaos…. I would speak [through music], and the world would listen.
 After this visionary experience, Sonny dropped out of college. He became obsessed with space travel and every manner of esoterica. He transformed the first floor of his family home in Alabama into a salon, where he would write, transcribe, record, and rehearse songs with local musicians and drifters passing through. A collective, a galaxy—Sonny at the center as others revolved around him—suited him well.
 When he was twenty-eight, Herman Blount was drafted to serve in World War II. He declared himself a conscientious objector on medical and moral grounds. When he failed to show up for the alternate service assigned to him, he got arrested. In court, Blount, representing himself, debated the judge on points of legal and biblical interpretation. When the judge remarked that he ought to be conscripted anyhow, Blount said he’d kill the first high-ranking military officer he met. The judge put him in Walker County Jail until further disposition of his case. “I’ve never seen a nigger like you before,” the judge said. “No,” Blount replied, “and you never will again.”
 After thirty-nine “unhappy and bewildered…almost crazed” days in jail, Sonny appealed to be sent to a Civilian Public Service camp. He ended up in Pennsylvania, where he practiced forestry during the day and played piano at night. Psychiatrists there described him as “a psychopathic personality,” prone to neurotic depression and sexual perversion, and as “a well-educated colored intellectual.” After a month or so he was designated unfit for military service (he was given a “4-F” for his hernia) and sent back to Birmingham.
 Two years later, Sonny headed to Chicago to reignite his musical career. He drank the Windy City down, its vibrant jazz and cabaret scenes, its Art Deco buildings and monuments, its convoluted history of Black Nationalism. Skipping from band to band, he finally formed his own. He called it the Arkestra. And on October 20, 1952, riffing on Egyptian mythology, Sonny renamed himself once more. He became Le Sony’r Ra, or Sun Ra for short. Herman Blount, he said, was a slave name.
 Pictures of Sun Ra often suggest chaotic hybridity: priestly futuristic costumes and sets, ancient Egypt and the planet Saturn forming a palimpsest of past and future utopias. His sound synthesized big band, swing, hard bop, reggae, Afropop, electronic music, and Walt Disney musicals. His references—expressed in his lyrics, poetry, and pamphlets—showcased this eclecticism too: Kabbalah, gnosticism, freemasonry, pan-Africanism, Zen. When he taught a course at the University of California, Berkeley in 1971, his syllabus included  The Egyptian Book of the Dead; the theosophical works of Madame Blavatsky, the nineteenth-century Russian medium; Henry Dumas, a brilliant poet gunned down by New York City Transit Police in 1968. He often cited George G.M. James’s  Stolen Legacy (1954), which claimed that Greek philosophy had filched its ideas from Egyptian mythology.
 In Sun Ra’s various writings and interviews, he always maintained that there was a metaphysical basis for what he called his “equations”: non sequitur chains of koans and runes, of numerology and etymology. He had a bit of the guru’s antiphony of the individual and the collective to him. Sun Ra was always gathering disciples, yet set himself apart from them. His biographer, John F. Szwed, quotes Sun Ra as saying, “I know what they’re talking about, but they don’t know what I’m talking about. I’m in the midst of what they’re doing but they’ve never been in the midst of what has been impressed upon my mind.” He was lone wolf and lupine leader, Anubis presiding over the vast nothing of the black world, the underworld, and outer space.
 In 1974 Sun Ra cowrote, produced, and starred in a film that’s often considered the origin text of what we now call Afrofuturism. The premise of  Space Is the Place returns us to his interplanetary vision: Sun Ra wants to “set up a colony for black people…. We’ll bring them here through  either  isotope teleportation, transmolecularization, or better still, teleport the whole planet here through music.”
 At one point, he sets up an Outer Space Employment Agency in Oakland, California, where he interviews Bay Area residents for the trip. Sun Ra sits behind a counter, wearing a silver helmet and a robe speckled with stars, trimmed in sparkle. A middle-aged black man approaches, wearing a trilby, a plaid jacket over a pointy collared shirt, and a necklace of beads and bones:
   Drunk: What is this? I mean, wh-like, uh, where am I? You know, uh, who is you?
  Drunk: Nothing?! Well, you better tell me about this nothin stuff cuz, uh (chuckle), I need a job. And I—and I ’on’t know what to do.
          Drunk: Nothin?! Nothin?! But I got to have somethin so I can get me another bottle. I can’t go for that shit…
 The applicant’s slang—“wh-like, uh, where am I? You know, uh, who is you?”—and Sun Ra’s Zen—“I’m everythin and nothin”—constitute a jazzy, contrapuntal metaphysics of blackness. Twenty-six years earlier, in his essay “Harlem Is Nowhere,” Ralph Ellison had found black people speaking the same richly metaphorical language: “In Harlem the reply to the greeting, ‘How are you?’ is very often, ‘Oh man, I’m  nowhere…’”
 Negro Americans are in desperate search for an identity. Rejecting the second-class status assigned them, they feel alienated and their whole lives have become a search for answers to the questions: Who am I, What am I, Why am I, and Where?
 This is by now a familiar negative theology of black identity. It also lies like a vacuum, a gyre, at the core of Afrofuturism. When the scholar Mark Dery first coined that term in 1992, he grounded it in absence, wondering at the seeming  lack of an Afrofuturist tradition:
 Why do so few African Americans write science fiction, a genre whose close encounters with the Other—the stranger in a strange land—would seem uniquely suited to the concerns of African-American novelists?… The notion of Afrofuturism gives rise to a troubling antinomy: Can a community whose past has been deliberately rubbed out, and whose energies have subsequently been consumed by the search for legible traces of its history, imagine possible futures?
 But there is a counterstrain to this nihilism in the black tradition as well: nothing can become everything, nowhere can become elsewhere. For Frantz Fanon in  Black Skin, White Masks,
 as soon as I  desire…I am not merely here-and-now, sealed into thingness. I am for somewhere else and for something else. I demand that notice be taken of my negating activity insofar as I pursue something other than life; insofar as I do battle for the creation of a human world.
 In  Space Is the Place, Sun Ra recruits black humans to accompany him to the elsewhere that is Saturn by appealing precisely to this shared ground of nonbeing. “I’m not real,” he says to a group of skeptical young men and women:
 I’m just like you. You don’t exist in this society. If you did, your people wouldn’t be seeking equal rights. You’re not real. If you were, you’d have some status among the nations of the world. So, we’re both myths. I do not come to you as a reality. I come to you as the myth. Because that’s what black people are, myths.
 Black matter itself—outer space, with all its velvety richness of the nil—becomes the  source of black energy, black futures. On the one hand, Sun Ra says, “the darkness. Nobody made that. It just happens. Light and all that—someone made that; it’s written that they did. But nobody made the darkness. My music is about dark tradition. Dark tradition means a lot more than black tradition.” On the other, Sun Ra named himself for the sun itself, that roiling source of a light that glows and splatters and bolts out of that universal darkness.
  Out of nowhere they come like    embers suddenly aflame      With living reach        Spiral infinity          Being.
   A beautiful golden nest Soft and shimmering with many    colors… Radiant like the sun. Yes, I have a nest; Out in outer space on the tip of    the worlds.
 He conjures with double negatives, like “Never is not,” and presses upon deictics like “this”:
  He multiplies the negative—“doubly no”—and flips it to “on”:
 The on-ness reach demands it be …rays to envelope them like a net    of kindly prisms… They will be, they will come forward to the two-wardness of the Intuition of the On
 His jazz, too, oscillates wildly, swerving from muddle to melody, riding dissonance into harmonics, skittering between sparkling notes and abrupt silence—the syncope that makes a rhythm a rhythm. Sun Ra’s art in all forms offers this challenge to black people: If we’re nothing, if we’re just myths, why not make that literal, why not make it material? Why not create, why not  become, glittering black matter?
  The photographer Ming Smith’s pictures of Sun Ra from 1978 beautifully capture this energy, this philosophy.  * Smith started her career in the Seventies as a model, which brought her in contact with many important black cultural figures of the era, including Alvin Ailey, James Baldwin, Grace Jones, Nina Simone, and Sun Ra. In 1973 Smith became the first woman to join Kamoinge, the Harlem-based photography collective; she was the first black woman photographer ever to have works acquired by  Mo MA. Smith’s photography is best known for its virtuosic blurring, for a vibrational quality that the music writer Greg Tate calls a “visual trill,” and for leveling foreground and background, yet delineating them through precise textural juxtaposition. She sometimes paints her photos with swirls and speckles that surface the prismatic colors  within blackness, a kind of precursor to Toyin Ojih Odutola’s drawings.
 What we might call Smith’s luxuriant, deconstructive chiaroscuro also arises from her technical innovations in lighting, shutter speed, and the relative movements of camera and subject—all of which derive from immersion, an insistence on taking pictures of black people in black spaces. As the artist Arthur Jafa puts it in a conversation that will appear with this essay in an Aperture volume on Smith’s work, “the technical parameters of what she’s doing are, in fact, structured by a commitment to being in those spaces that Black people occupy. The spaces that are underlit…secret spaces.” The jazz club milieu amps up this aesthetic of secret spaces, jostling bodies, and high contrast. Smith was married to the jazz musician David Murray for a time and accompanied him on tour; their son Mingus is also a musician.
 In Smith’s many photographs of black musicians with brass instruments in underlit spaces, chiaroscuro is abstracted and intensified. This isn’t black and white, but dark and shine; we’re not just blending and bleeding and blurring, but shifting and shaking and shuddering. Shine stammers through degrees across the dark, leaving prismatic trails, flame-like scribbles and warps. The result is less of a glissando across absence and presence and more of a rhythm—a syncopated scatting of them. This is how Ming Smith evokes the precision, uncanniness, and joy of Sun Ra’s metaphysics of bright blackness.
 In her photograph  Sun Ra Space II, light doesn’t just outline him, it constitutes him. Sun Ra  is the spotlight, a shape-shifting shimmer, yet also a monument. His suit has turned to imbricated stone, topped by the swinging shine of cape, headdress, and sunglasses—which seem literal, glasses made of sun. Their shape is echoed by the electric bulb above and inverted by the player to the right, who wears shades limned with light. A reflection casts a third eye on Sun Ra’s forehead, bright and twisty—a wonky antenna or the keys of the brass instruments being played by the members of his Arkestra. They are a pale clutter of lines and shapes behind him, their round hats counterpoints to the dark circles on the wall on the left—again like an instrument, like the keyholes of a giant flute. The light is metallic, the shadows articulated—jolted, jarred. The scene is skew, backed by the grid of a ceiling glimpsed mid-fall, reminding us that Smith is there, moving. Sun Ra, too, is moving, mugging for us—semiserious, deadly dignified—as he turns, his cape the flung arm of a galaxy. But he’s also somehow perfectly still, the eye of the storm, the center of the spiral.
 In  Sun Ra Space I, he’s facing away, pure surface. We see the wingspan of his cape, the back of his head framed with textures: a scratched floor on one side, a machine-like honeycomb on the other, and a white oblong with dark windows—like a train or a façade—above. In the center of the photo, from the coarse bright orb of his headdress, Sun Ra’s back rays out and down, grains of light strewn like stars, tumbling down pleated dunes. Light and dark are atomized, adrift: the moonlit sands of Egypt, a murky constellation. Seen together, the two-wardness of the photos suggests a gestalt shift, Sun Ra mid-turn, Sun Ra turned away, sheer flight, sheer energy—sheerness itself: “I could see through myself. And I went up.” Smith’s dyad thus conveys the lightness of Sun Ra’s wit, his wonder: the trickster spins and vanishes, scatters into the air.
 Jafa observes that, in many of Smith’s photos, “you can’t identify anybody” because she embraces “techniques that in a sense void the ability of the photograph to function as evidence.” There’s no doubt this is Sun Ra, though, with all the lapidary aura that beams off filmic footage of him—“the stones speak…through vibrations of beauty,” Sun Ra says of Egyptian hieroglyphs in the 1980 documentary  Sun Ra: A Joyful Noise. Here, Smith’s vanishing quality doesn’t disguise or erase him, but rather  inheres in his manifest presence. We feel it in the spin, the lapping layers, the granular sheerness, the constellatory cape and spectral community. Sun Ra is there and not there, just as Smith is there and not there: “I’m everythin and nothin.” Rather than relegating the negation of blackness to incoherence or pessimism, these two artists dare us to reimagine black identity—that is, human identity—from the groundless ground up, as an order of being that stutters in and out of nonbeing, that dissolves and gathers itself and others, in turn, in time.
 Did Sun Ra truly believe he had once been transmolecularized to Saturn? Did he really want to save black people by sending them to outer space? Was he some kind of intergalactic Marcus Garvey, who sold tickets back to Africa but never set sail? Was he pulling an elaborate prank? Does it matter? Sun Ra never broke character, never said “it was all a joke” or “it was just a scam” or “this world has made me mad.” With his strategic omissions, he turned a “nigger” into “a colored intellectual” into a god; alchemized gold out of thin air; became a tall story, a living myth, a human among humans, a kindly prism. This is what Ming Smith’s dazzling dark photographs of Herman Blount, Sonny, Le Sony’r Ra, Sun Ra, disclose. He’s the boy who cried wolf, he’s the wolf itself, he’s the shimmering go-between.
    *  This essay will appear, in somewhat different form, in  Ming Smith: An Aperture Monograph, to be published by Aperture and Documentary Arts in October 2020.  ↩
title: 孙拉：“我是一切，什么都不是。”

note: 当我无家可归的时候，我在某个论坛上的个人资料中写道，我是独立贫穷的。这不仅仅是开玩笑的幽默。虽然我无家可归，但我有赡养费，我做自由职业，我有一定程度的代理能力，这在穷人中是很少见的。我能够安排我的生活，在一定程度上通过做零工来优先照顾我的健康(这篇文章是专门为我运营的论坛r/GigWorks撰写的)。换句话说，我有一定程度的独立性和中介性，这通常只有在喷气式飞机上才能看到--那些被称为独立富裕的人--或者舒适富裕的退休人员。我比你们这些典型的工作狂更能掌控我的时间和生活。我能够做到这一点，部分原因是我在财务方面相当精明。我认为，任何人在精神上和后勤上都可以到达同样的地方，即使你目前没有太多钱。这在很大程度上与给你的大脑灌输正确的东西有关，以便改变你与金钱的精神和情感关系。长期以来，我一直在收集一些关于金钱以及人们如何与之相关的心理片段。例如，(具有足够讽刺意味的)早在我的医疗诊断之前，我就在脑海中记下了这样一个想法：如果你患有致命的疾病，而且无法治愈，再多的钱也解决不了你的问题。我也真的很喜欢“泰坦尼克号”中的场景，富人试图用钱买到救生艇，却被告知他的钱在海底一文不值。随着我财务问题的加深，我开始更深入地阅读和思考金钱以及人们如何与之相关的话题。我学到了去你妈的钱这句话，这是我以前从未听说过的。它是有足够的钱才能告诉任何人下地狱的想法，这要归功于汉弗莱·鲍嘉(参见下面的链接)。读到他的政策，手头有足够的钱，这样他就可以挑选自己的角色，这让我意识到，他挑剔的能力很可能是他拥有如此传奇的演员声誉的原因：他从来没有为了避免挨饿而选择糟糕的角色。因此，他从来没有因为在糟糕的电影中扮演糟糕的角色而出名。于是，一个传说诞生了。由于离婚和医疗危机，我的财务状况一落千丈，我对此想了很多。从某种意义上说，我年轻的时候从来没有这样的奢侈。我十几岁的时候结婚是为了逃离家人，因为我曾被亲戚猥亵过。这鼓励了我的丈夫将我视为无助和无能，作为拒绝我选择自由的借口，尽管我是毕业班的尖子生之一，并获得了国家功勋奖学金。他还是表现得像是把我从救济金之类的地方救了出来。我最终在非常困难的情况下离开了他，这再次限制了我的选择自由。一路走来，我得出的结论是，我不会说操你的话是我生活中非常糟糕的一个重要原因，如果我想让自己的生活恢复正常，我必须改正这一点。最终，我得出的结论是，能够做出选择实际上不是为了钱本身，而是为了回旋余地。是的，拥有足够的资源是有帮助的，但在生活中做出选择和行使自由意志并不直接与一个人有多少钱或少钱有直接关系。如果你有一些信心，你不会挨饿或经历灾难，这会有所帮助，但这不是钱的问题。首先，拥有一个花园并知道如何狩猎可以保证你不会挨饿，即使你一文不名。另一方面，有时人们会选择操你妈的选择，即使他们没有任何保证能活下来。当我读到“戒酒者之书”的第一章时，我对这一点的理解变得清晰起来，而当时这第一章在网上是免费的。第一章名为“不要辞去白天的工作”。黑客新闻在本质上建议你，为了保持回旋的余地，保留你的日常工作。免费的在线章节清楚地表明，如果你放弃了日常工作，没有真正的积蓄，你会突然发现自己在谈判交易时处于劣势，发现自己接受了你真的不想要的交易，只想得到一些你不想要的东西。这一章清楚地表明，如果你放弃了白天的工作，没有真正的积蓄，你就会突然发现自己在谈判交易时处于劣势，发现自己接受了你真的不想要的交易。在我无家可归的时候，有一段时间，亲戚们帮我处理了一些金融闹剧，然后他们觉得有权因为这件事而试图管理我的生活。例如，我被指示关闭我的银行账户，并将其转移到其他地方。他们没有提出要求，也没有建议这是一个帮助我保护自己免受债权人伤害的好主意。他们把它作为法令发布，好像他们知道什么是对我最好的，好像他们有权决定我的选择，因为他们帮助了我，而我处于非常脆弱的状态。我能够顶住这样的压力，自己做决定，包括把我的银行账户转移到
note_en:
While I was homeless, I had a descriptor in my profile on some forum that I was "Independently Poor." That was more than just tongue-in-cheek humor.  Although I was homeless, I had alimony and I was doing freelance work and I had a degree of agency that you rarely see among poor people. I was able to arrange my life to prioritize taking care of my health in part by doing gig work (and this post is being written expressly for  r/GigWorks, a forum I run).   In other words, I had a degree of independence and agency that you normally only see in the Jet Set -- people who get called  independengly wealthy -- or comfortably well-off retirees. I had more control over my time and my life than your typical working stiff.  I was able to get to that point in part because I'm pretty financially savvy. I think anyone can get to that same place mentally and logistically, even if you don't currently have much money. A lot of it has to do with feeding your mind the right stuff in order to change your mental and emotional relationship to money.  I have long collected little mental snippets about money and how people relate to it. For example, (ironically enough) long before my medical diagnosis, I made a mental note of the idea that if you have a deadly disease and there is no cure, no amount of money will fix your problem.   I also really like the scene in  Titanic where the rich guy tries to buy his way into a lifeboat and gets told his money won't be worth anything at the bottom of the ocean. As my financial problems deepened, I began reading and thinking more deeply on the topic of money and how people relate to it. I learned the phrase "fuck you money" which I had not heard before. It is the idea of having enough money "to be able to tell anyone to go to hell" and it is attributed to Humphrey Bogart (see links below).  Reading about his policy to have enough money on hand so he could pick and choose his roles made me realize that his ability to be choosy is likely why he has such a legendary reputation as an actor: He never had to take lousy roles to keep from starving. Therefore, he never got known for playing bad roles in bad movies. Thus, a legend was born.  As my finances tanked due to a combination of divorce and medical crisis, I thought a lot about that. In some sense, I never had that luxury when I was younger.   I got married in my teens to escape my family because I had been molested by relatives. This encouraged my husband to view me as helpless and incompetent as an excuse to deny me freedom of choice, even though I was one of the top students in my graduating class and had won a National Merit Scholarship. He still acted like he saved me from welfare or something.   I eventually left him under very difficult circumstances which again curtailed my freedom of choice. Somewhere along the way, I concluded that my lack of ability to say "fuck you" is a big part of what went so very wrong with my life and I have to fix that if I am ever going to get my life straightened out.  Ultimately, I concluded that being able to choose wasn't really about money per se but was about  maneuvering room. Yes, having sufficient resources helps, but making choices in life and exercising free will is not directly related to how much or how little money a person has.   It helps if you have some confidence you won't starve or otherwise experience catastrophe, but that isn't about money per se. For one thing, having a garden and knowing how to hunt can assure you that you won't starve, even if you don't have a dime. For another, sometimes people take the "fuck you" choice even when there is zero assurance they will survive it.  My understanding of that was crystallized when I read the first chapter of the  "The Quitter Book" at a time when that first chapter was available online for free. The first chapter is called "Don't Quit Your Day Job."   It was recommended on Hacker News in essence to say "keep your day job in order to keep your maneuvering room." The free online chapter stated clearly that if you give up your day job and have no real savings, you suddenly find yourself negotiating deals from a position of weakness and find yourself accepting deals you really don't want just to have  something coming in.  At one time while I was homeless, relatives helped me deal with some of my financial drama and they then felt entitled to try to run my life because of it. For example, I was instructed to close my bank account and move it elsewhere.   They didn't ask and they didn't suggest it as a good idea to help me protect myself from my creditors. They issued it as an edict like they knew what was best for me and like they had a right to dictate my choices because they had helped me and I was in a very vulnerable state.  I was able to resist such pressures and make decisions for myself, including moving my bank account at a later date on terms that made more sense for me, because I had some maneuvering room in the form of food stamps and knowing where I could get a free meal or free clothes. Food stamps didn't cover some essential nonfood items that we needed and couldn't get from a homeless services center, specifically peroxide and plastic cups, but we made more of an effort to get better at finding change on the street and we used that to buy those essentials at some local "dollar" store.   I blogged about that while I was going through the worst of it and part of what I said was:  I have 35¢ in change and a dollar in "rewards bucks" from a store that sells plastic cups. I think $1.34 will give me "fuck you" money for this week. I think we will be fine.  I also think I finally have enough real say about my own life to start getting it straightened out, never mind that I am living on the streets.
  If you are currently either homeless or in dire straits, you can establish your own maneuvering room and foster your own "FU, Money" attitude by following some of these steps: Come up with a plan for how to make money doing something that makes sense for you and actively pursue that, even if it means eating at soup kitchens for a while.
  Work like hell to meet your real needs in spite of the budgetary constraints. This is the antidote to a  poverty mentality and will help you find a path forward. This includes but is not limited  finding ways to keep yourself adequately fed, even if you have no money.
    I wrote a post on some blog of mine titled "Money and Maneuvering Room" on 2012/07/12. It was copied and pasted to another blog of mine in 2014, retitled, updated a bit and posted to Hacker News,  where it did surprisingly well. This is its latest incarnation and has been edited and updated substantially, but some parts of this are very much recycled content.  If you can spare some money, please  leave a tip or support my  Patreon. Supporting my work doesn't just pay my bills, it also helps me to help others who are currently homeless or at risk of homelessness by providing useful information of a sort not readily found elsewhere.   And right now is a particularly bad time to be homeless. Many things are closed due to the pandemic, including libraries and other services that matter more than average to the poorest of the poor in America.  A huge THANK YOU to my current Patreon supporters and anyone who has ever left me a tip.
title: 独立贫穷：傅家富货币的一个转折--也就是。“福，钱”
title_en: Independently Poor: A Twist on FU Money – a.k.a. “FU, Money”

note: 到底是谁在掌管我们的思想呢？我们在一个引人入胜的三个部分的旅程中考虑证据，看看我们是否对自己的决定负责。从神经科学到物理学，再到我们对自由意志的理解如何影响我们的道德和我们所做的选择。
note_en: Who is really in charge of our minds? We consider the evidence in a fascinating three part journey into whether or not we are in charge of our own decisions. From neuroscience to physics to how our understanding of free will impacts on our morality and the choices we make.
title: 物理学表明我们没有自由意志
title_en: The physics that suggests we have no free will

note:
苹果上个月推出了新的“查找我的网络附件程序”，旨在允许第三方产品与苹果自己的‌“查找我的‌”应用程序配合使用。虽然苹果的AirTags尚未正式发布，但这一计划被视为苹果与Tile等蓝牙定位追踪器竞争的一种方式，避免了苹果垄断市场的指控。
在“华盛顿邮报”今天的一篇新报道中，该计划还有更多以前没有详细介绍过的方面，包括对使用‌Find My‌应用程序的第三方公司制定更严格的规则。一位匿名开发者分享了苹果关于‌Find My‌的一份长达50页的秘密文件，他表示，使用苹果应用程序定位设备的客户将被禁止同时使用第三方服务。
尽管细节仍然很少，但这表明，尽管你可以将磁贴追踪器链接到‌Find My‌，并使用苹果的应用程序来定位丢失的钱包，但你将无法使用Tile自己的应用程序来做同样的事情。此外，由于苹果对始终允许定位的限制，每一家外部公司都必须向每位苹果用户征得许可才能获得他们的位置，这对物品定位应用来说是一个明显的障碍。
开发者指出的另一个问题是，他们接触iPhone的蓝牙天线和其他苹果硬件的机会有限。虽然‌Find My‌应用程序可以在需要的时候使用这些硬件，但第三方软件只能在一定的阈值内使用蓝牙天线，如果开发人员超过了这个阈值，苹果就会切断他们的访问权限，并阻止软件工作。值得注意的是，根据这些开发者的说法，苹果没有告诉他们具体的门槛是什么。
在苹果的‌Find My‌应用程序发布后，在苹果自己的蓝牙跟踪硬件的传言中，Tile开始对苹果采取行动。该公司在5月份发给欧盟的一封信中指控苹果滥用权力，非法偏袒自己的产品。Tile表示，苹果有选择地关闭了允许无缝用户体验的功能，这使得用户在iOS设备上操作Tile蓝牙追踪器变得更加困难。
尽管开发者在今天的“华盛顿邮报”上发表了声明，但苹果公司发言人亚历克斯·基尔施纳表示，苹果公司认为其‌Find My‌网络配件计划对缺乏建立位置搜索服务资源的小公司有帮助：如果你是一个对进入搜索空间感兴趣的小公司，而你还没有建立搜索网络，这就允许你这样做。苹果否认其政策是反竞争的。
一点儿没错。我们最不需要的就是一个Android类型的系统，它最终会超出我们赋予它的权限来监视和跟踪我们。据一位[S]匿名开发者[/S]说，他分享了一份来自Tile公司牢骚满腹的首席执行官苹果公司(Apple)长达50页的秘密PDF文档。
位置数据极其敏感。我现在对这种情况还不够了解，不能说我站在哪一边...。但我认为，如果苹果整体上不严格，我肯定会担心。
哈哈，这是苹果的精明之处。我不认为他们期望任何人真的加入这个计划：商品寻找市场并不是什么大规模的、蓬勃发展的现象，所以他们提出的针对初创公司的计划只是一种侮辱，强调从他们的角度来看，这根本不是一个独立的市场。虽然Find My应用程序可以在需要的时候使用这些硬件，但第三方软件只能在一定的阈值内使用蓝牙天线，如果开发人员超过了这个阈值，苹果就会切断他们的访问权限，并阻止软件工作。值得注意的是，根据这些开发者的说法，苹果没有告诉他们具体的门槛是什么。
这就是为什么Tile不能开发自己的应用程序，而苹果的应用程序可以做到这一点。在声称他们贪婪或对苹果提出任何特殊要求之前，请先阅读这篇文章。从理论上讲，他们所要求的是完全合理的--能够在苹果的操作系统上编写他们自己的类似好的应用程序。这里似乎没有人记得微软曾试图通过将Internet Explorer与Windows捆绑在一起来锁定用户。这一举动最终伤害了整个互联网，因为IE是如此糟糕，尤其是因为Mac电脑没有IE，所以网站无法正常工作或看起来很糟糕(最终作为微软交易IIRC的一部分获得了IE)。还记得美国的移动运营商因为要求我们都使用他们的支付服务而屏蔽移动支付系统的时候吗？这里也有类似的情况。苹果是一家硬件和操作系统开发商，他正在利用这一地位赋予自己的产品超强的能力，这是其他产品无法比拟的。它压倒了竞争，对消费者来说完全是垃圾。
应该强制Tile编写一款应用程序，让任何公司的设备都能在使用它的情况下被发现。这将使竞争环境变得公平，这正是瓷砖想要的。非常出色!。
联邦政府不会高兴的，除非他们迫使苹果公司让他们的平台对隐私窃贼和诈骗软件如此开放，以至于政府的跟踪和窥探将是微不足道的，不会被注意到。
我这里两边都有。对于苹果来说，你想要的是审计Find My系统中所有参与者的安全性。但是，如果你重新磁贴，你就是在与一款默认的、由系统自动安装的应用程序竞争。一旦苹果发布了一个瓦片竞争对手，他们将拥有可以被体面地认为是不公平的优势。解决方案：苹果应该致力于创建一款FindKit&34；允许其他公司满足一定的规格，以便在Find My应用程序中展开竞争。通过有选择地禁用允许无缝用户体验的功能。&#34；这句话几乎可以用于操作系统为提高安全性而采取的几乎任何操作。你堵住的每一个洞，都是一些应用程序用来提供某些功能的途径。
问题是，苹果在什么是操作系统和什么是应用程序之间划出了一条灰色界线。Find My是一款来自苹果的应用程序，它可能必须在公平的竞争环境中竞争。除非苹果能为竞争对手提供进入FindMy应用程序的方法，否则竞争对手会怨声载道。你不喜欢吗？做你自己的系统。苹果可以随心所欲地运行自己的软件/硬件。
苹果应该被迫像所有竞争对手一样，在自己的应用商店中展开竞争。
苹果公司今天上午发布了iOS和iPadOS14的第三个开发者测试版，用于测试目的，对更新中的一些功能和设计变化进行了调整和完善。随着测试期的进行，变化会变得不那么明显，但第三个测试版中有一些值得注意的新功能，我们在下面重点介绍了这些功能。-新的音乐图标-苹果推出了更新的...

note_en:
Apple last month  introduced the new " Find My" Network Accessory Program, built to allow third-party products to work with Apple's own ‌Find My‌ app. While Apple's  AirTags have yet to be formally announced, this program was seen as a way for Apple to level the playing field with competing Bluetooth location trackers, like Tile, and avoid accusations of Apple monopolizing the market.
 In a new report today by   The Washington Post, there are more aspects to this program that haven't been previously detailed, including far stricter rules for third-party companies using the ‌Find My‌ app. According to an anonymous developer who shared a secret 50-page PDF from Apple about ‌Find My‌, customers who use Apple's app to locate a device will be barred from using third-party services simultaneously.
 Although the details remain sparse, this suggests that while you will be able to link a Tile tracker to ‌Find My‌ and use Apple's app to locate a lost wallet, for example, you would then be prevented from using Tile's own app to do the same. Additionally, because of Apple's restrictions to "always allow" location access, every outside company will have to ask each Apple user for permission to obtain their location, which is a notable hindrance for item location apps.
 Another issue pointed out by developers is their limited access to the  iPhone's Bluetooth antenna and other Apple hardware. While the ‌Find My‌ app can use these pieces of hardware whenever it needs to, third-party software can only use the Bluetooth antenna within certain thresholds, and if the developers go beyond that Apple cuts their access off and prevents the software from working. Notably, according to these developers, Apple does not inform them what the specific threshold is.
 Following the announcement of Apple's ‌Find My‌ app and amid the  rumors swirling of Apple's own Bluetooth tracking hardware, Tile began taking action against Apple. The company  accused Apple of abuse of power and of illegally favoring its own products in a letter sent to the European Union in May. Tile said that Apple was making it more difficult for users to operate the Tile Bluetooth trackers on iOS devices, "by selectively disabling features that allow for seamless user experience."
 Despite the claims made by developers in  The Washington Post today, Apple spokesman Alex Kirschner said that the company sees its ‌Find My‌ Network Accessory Program as helpful to smaller companies that lack resources to build a location-finding service: "If you were a smaller player interested in getting into the finding space and you haven’t built a finding network, this allows you to do that." Apple has denied that its policies are anticompetitive.
 Exactly. the last thing we need is an Android type of system that ends up watching and following us beyond the permissions we give it.   " According to an [S]anonymous developer [/S]who shared a secret 50-page PDF from Apple "   The whiny CEO of Tile.
 Location data is extremely sensitive. I don’t know enough about this situation right now to say which side I’m on... but I think I would certainly be worried if Apple wasn’t been strict as a whole.
 lol this is shrewd from Apple. I don’t think they expect anybody to actually join this program: the item-finding market isn’t some massive, booming phenomenon, so their proposed program targeted at startups is just an insult to emphasise that it really isn’t a separate market at all from their perspective.   While the Find My app can use these pieces of hardware whenever it needs to, third-party software can only use the Bluetooth antenna within certain thresholds, and if the developers go beyond that Apple cuts their access off and prevents the software from working. Notably, according to these developers, Apple does not inform them what the specific threshold is.
This is why Tile can’t make their own app which does what Apple’s can. Read the article before claiming they’re greedy or demanding anything special from Apple. In theory what they’re asking for is perfectly reasonable - the  ability to write a similarly-good App of their own on Apple’s OS.  It’s like nobody here remembers Microsoft trying to lock people in to Internet Explorer by tying it deeply in to Windows. That was a move that ended up hurting the entire internet because IE was so crap, and hurt Apple especially because Macs didn’t have IE so websites didn’t work properly or looked bad (they eventually got it as part of the Microsoft deal IIRC). Or remember when the cellular operators in the US were blocking mobile payment systems because they were demanding we all use  their payment service?  it’s a similar thing here. Apple is a hardware and operating system developer who is using that position to grant their own products superpowers that no other product can match. It crushes competition and is totally crappy for consumers.
 Tile should be forced to write an app that allows any company's devices to be found using it. That would level the playing field, exactly what tile wants. Brilliant!
 The Feds are not going to be happy until they have forced Apple to make their platform so open to privacy thieves and scamware that government tracking and snooping will be trivial and unnoticed.
 I get both sides here. For Apple, you want the security of auditing all your participants in the Find My system.  But, if you're tile, you are competing with an app that is the default and installed by the system automatically. Once Apple releases a tile competitor, they will have what can decently be considered an unfair advantage.  Solution: Apple should work to create a "FindKit" which allows for other companies to meet a certain specification to allow competition in the "Find My" app.   "by selectively disabling features that allow for seamless user experience."  That sentence can be used for almost any action an OS takes to increase security. Every hole you plug, is an avenue that some app out there was using to provide some functionality.
The problem is, Apple creates a grey line between "what is the OS" and "what is an App". Find My is an App from Apple that should probably have to compete on an even playing field. Unless Apple can provide a way for competitors to make it into the FindMy app, the competitors have a gripe.   Don't like it? Make your own system. Apple can run their own software/hardware however they want.
Apple should be forced to compete in its own App Store like all of the competitors do.

note:
在皮埃达山建筑中发现了阿萨亚卡特宫的遗迹和一座由科尔特修道会建造的房屋。
*城市考古计划在1469年至1481年期间发现了与Tenochtitlan的Axayácatl，Tatoani的老房子中的一个开放广场相对应的玄武岩平板地板。
*INAH的考古打捞项目还发现了征服者住所的遗迹，这里后来成为新西班牙第一卡比尔多和瓦哈卡谷侯爵的总部。
在Axayácatl老房子的围墙内，发生了征服墨西哥-Tenochtitlán过程中一些最具决定性的事件，例如Tallatoani Moctezuma Xocoyotzin的死亡，可能是在1520年6月29日；意想不到的结果破坏了墨西哥人和西班牙人之间的关系，并引发了公开对抗。
国家人类学与历史研究所(INAH)的专家最近发现了西班牙前宫殿的遗迹，以及埃尔南·科尔特(Hernán Cortés)下令建造的后来的房屋遗迹，这些遗迹使用了以前特诺奇卡(Tenochca)建筑的重复使用材料，唤起了人们对五个世纪后那些历史事件的记忆。
多亏了历史资料，考古学家们并不神秘，在位于大都会大教堂(Metropolitan Cathedral)旁边的墨西哥城历史中心最具标志性的建筑之一皮亚达山(Nacional Monte De Piedad)的地板下，是阿萨亚卡特尔宫的地基，当他们到达特诺切蒂特兰市中心时，阿萨亚卡特尔宫就是他们的生活区。
在过去的二十年里，利用在该机构总部进行的各种改建项目的优势，各种INAH专家发现了莫克特祖马二世父亲故居的遗迹。最新的发现是在城市考古项目(PAU)负责人劳尔·巴雷拉·罗德里格斯(Raúl Barrera Rodríguez)和合作者何塞·玛丽亚·加西亚·格雷罗(JoséMaría García Guerrero)领导的考古干预期间曝光的。
在对这座历史建筑的北半部分进行了整修检查后，PAU人员在2017年9月初至2018年8月中旬期间进行了一项考古打捞项目，重点是主院和相邻的一个房间。值得注意的是，该项目在挖掘后研究阶段继续进行到现在。
挖掘发现了许多玄武岩楼板，这些楼板肯定是阿萨亚卡特尔宫一个开放广场的一部分，阿萨亚卡特尔宫是1469年至1481年间控制特诺奇蒂特兰命运的统治者。
此外，从早期的总督时期(公元1521-1620年)，一座由科尔特修道会建造的房子的遗迹被发现。西班牙征服者在那里居住了几年，这座建筑也将成为新西班牙的第一个卡比尔多(1525年左右)和四年后授予征服者的瓦哈卡谷侯爵的所在地。
作为公众宣传计划的一个激动人心的部分&#34；Contigo en en la Distancia“(”与你远方同行“)，来自文化部的PAU负责人劳尔·巴雷拉(Raúl Barrera)和他的合作者何塞·玛丽亚·加西亚(JoséMaría García)解释说，他们在皮埃达山国家大庭院周围挖掘了12个测试坑-每侧2米，深1.5米，正在进行改建工作，以加固定义结构的柱子。
通过挖掘北侧、东侧和西侧的一系列试验坑，发现了一堵高1.50米、宽1.25米的石砌墙的遗迹。这面墙是1755年前后建造的一系列柱子的基础和基础平台，这让我们可以推断中央庭院最初更大。此外，在同一空间的西段还发现了底层地板：底部和一根其他柱子的竖井，这些柱子来自早期的维多利亚州时期。
然而，在隔壁的房间里等待着重大的惊喜。PAU的考古学家有机会进行了广泛的挖掘，这使得他们能够找到一个由玄武岩和Tezontle(泡状熔岩)装饰的石头制成的房间的遗迹-5米乘4米-其底部位于玄武岩板的地板上。进一步的分析得出结论，一旦1521年墨西哥-特诺奇蒂特兰陷落，这里就是赫尔南·科特的故乡。
在Cortés房子的底板下，超过3米深，发现了另一层玄武岩板的遗迹，但来自前西班牙裔时代。根据它的特点，专家们推断它是阿萨亚卡特尔宫前的一个开放空间的一部分，很可能是一个庭院。
巴雷拉和加西亚强调，新生的总督时代的遗迹与阿萨亚卡特尔老房子里的再利用材料相对应。这些建筑，就像特诺奇蒂特兰圣地的许多其他建筑一样，几乎被西班牙人和他们的土著盟友摧毁了，几乎到了他们的地基。
例如，在殖民地房间东南内角的正面，发现了两块前西班牙裔装饰的石头，上面雕刻着高浮雕的雕塑。它们描绘了一条有羽毛的蛇(Quetzalcóatl)和一个羽毛头饰，肯定属于阿萨亚卡特尔宫殿的一个嵌板。此外，另一座带有象征天奎兹特利(Tianquiztli)或市场的字形的墨西哥雕塑被找回，取而代之的是它作为竖井的一部分。
对这些发现进行初步年代测定的一些诊断材料是西班牙裔前和殖民地时期的陶器遗迹，其类型(风格)属于上述历史时期：阿兹特克III：橙色单色、橙上黑色和德克萨斯可可抛光陶器；以及釉面陶器和西班牙和新西班牙马约利卡陶器。
劳尔·巴雷拉(Raúl Barrera)也是INAH抢救考古理事会的研究员，他反思了这样一个事实，即一些物证非常缺乏，足以说明问题。最近在国家皮埃达山的发现就是这样的情况，这些发现“表明”特诺赫蒂特兰的主要建筑受到了破坏，无论是出于象征意义还是实际目的。
尽管遗骸的性质不起眼，但西班牙入侵者及其盟友在1519年11月8日抵达特诺奇蒂特兰时踩过的那些前西班牙裔地板。莫克特祖马二世允许他们住在属于他父亲的老房子里，不久之后，他们把这些房子变成了他们的营房。
在那座宫殿里，发生了许多事情。例如，他们重新装修了一个房间来庆祝弥撒，就在那里，他们还囚禁了各种统治者，从他们杰出的东道主莫克特祖马·索科尤津(Moctezuma Xocoyotzin)，以及伊兹塔帕拉帕勋爵库特拉瓦克(Cuitláhuac)，特斯科科的卡卡马津(Cacamatzin)，特拉塔尼(Tlatoani)，以及特拉特洛尔科(Tlatelolco)的伊茨库赫津(Itzcuauhtzin)等人开始。
继1520年5月22日在圣殿市长(“大庙宇”)举行的托克斯卡特尔节期间发生的大屠杀之后，西班牙人奉佩德罗·德·阿尔瓦拉多的命令撤退并返回阿萨亚卡特尔宫。这一事件激起了墨西哥人的愤怒，同年6月底，他们围攻了那座大楼里的入侵者。Hernán Cortés想要通过Moctezuma来控制他们的愤怒，Moctezuma试图在宫殿的屋顶上安抚他们。但这些努力都是徒劳的，因为他的哥哥奎特拉瓦克已经被任命为新的统治者。
1520年6月30日晚，西班牙人通过特拉科潘(今天的塔库巴)的堤道逃到了特拉克斯卡拉，留下了阿萨亚卡特尔的老房子。
在Tenochtitlan陷落后，幸存的墨西哥人被迫亲手摧毁他们的寺庙和宫殿，并用同样的材料建造新城市。Axayácatl的老房子将让位于Hernán de Cortés的房子，这也是1525年左右新西班牙第一次卡比尔多的临时总部，以及1529年授予的瓦哈卡谷侯爵(Marquessate Of The Marquessate Of The Aaxaca Valley)，这是一个负责创建政府的崇高头衔，以表彰他为西班牙王室服务。
1547年征服者死后，他的后代保留了所有权。他的儿子马丁·科尔特斯·苏尼加(Martín Cortés Zúñiga)继承了侯爵王位，但在1566年，他卷入了一场企图夺取新西班牙政府的阴谋，因此被总督开除。这些房屋和大片土地仍然掌握在一名法律代表手中，而且已经状况不佳，于1836年被卖给了萨克罗·蒙特·德·皮达德(Sacro Monte De Piedad)。
note_en:
Remains of the Palace of Axayácatl and a house built by order of Cortés are discovered in Monte de Piedad building
  *** The Urban Archeology Program uncovered basalt slab floors corresponding to an open plaza in the Old Houses of Axayácatl,  tlatoani of Tenochtitlan between 1469 and 1481.
    *** INAH’s archaeological salvage project also detected the remains of the conqueror's dwelling, which later became the headquarters of the first Cabildo of New Spain and of the Marquessate of the Valley of Oaxaca.
      Within the walls of the Old Houses of Axayácatl some of the most decisive episodes of the conquest of Mexico-Tenochtitlán occurred, such as the death of the  tlatoani Moctezuma Xocoyotzin, possibly on June 29, 1520; unexpected outcomes that undermined the relationship between Mexicans and Spaniards and triggered the open confrontation.
    The most recent discovery made by specialists from the National Institute of Anthropology and History (INAH), of vestiges of that pre-Hispanic palace, and of the remains of a later house built under the orders of Hernán Cortés, with reused materials from the previous Tenochca building, revives the memory of those historical events, five centuries later.
    Thanks to historical sources, it is no mystery for archaeologists that under the floors of the Nacional Monte de Piedad, one of the most emblematic buildings of Mexico City’s Historic Center, located next to the Metropolitan Cathedral, are the foundations of the Palace of Axayácatl, which functioned as the living quarters of Cortés' entourage upon their arrival in the heart of Tenochtitlan.
    Over the last two decades, and taking advantage of diverse remodeling projects undertaken in the headquarters of that institution, various INAH experts have found remnants of the former residence of Moctezuma II's father. The most recent discoveries were brought to light during archaeological interventions led by Raúl Barrera Rodríguez and José María García Guerrero, head and collaborator, respectively, of the Urban Archeology Program (PAU).
    Following an inspection for refurbishments conducted in the northern half of the historic building, PAU personnel carried out an archaeological salvage project between the first days of September 2017 and mid-August 2018, focusing on the main courtyard and an adjacent room. It is noteworthy that the project continues to the present in the post-dig research phase.
    Excavations resulted in the discovery of numerous basalt slab floors, which must have formed part of an open plaza in the Palace of Axayácatl, the ruler who controlled the destiny of Tenochtitlan between 1469 and 1481.
    Also, from the early Viceregal period (1521-1620 AD), the remains of a house built by order of Cortés were uncovered. That construction, where the Spanish conqueror resided for several years, would also become the seat of the first Cabildo of New Spain (around 1525) and of the Marquessate of the Valley of Oaxaca, granted to the conqueror four years later.
        As an exciting part of the public outreach program "Contigo en la Distancia” (“With you from a Distance”), from the Ministry of Culture, the head of the PAU, Raúl Barrera, and his collaborator José María García, explain that they dug 12 test pits - 2 meters per side and 1.50 m deep— around the main courtyard of the National Monte de Piedad, where remodeling work was being carried out to reinforce the columns that define the structure and support the first level of the building.
    By excavating a series of test pits, on the north, east and west sides, the remains of a stone masonry wall - 1.50 meters high by 1.25 m wide - were uncovered. This wall served as a foundation and basal platform for a series of columns of the building erected around 1755, which allows us to deduce that the central courtyard was originally larger. Also, in the west section of this same space were found the subflooring: basal portions and a shaft of other columns from the early Viceregal period.
    However, major surprises awaited in the adjacent room. PAU archaeologists had the opportunity to conduct an extensive excavation, which allowed them to locate the remains of a room made of basalt and  tezontle (vesicular lava) dressed stones - 5 by 4 meters -, whose base sat upon a floor of basalt slabs. Further analyses led to the conclusion that this was the home of Hernán Cortés, once Mexico-Tenochtitlan fell in 1521.
    Below the subflooring of the house of Cortés, more than 3 meters deep, the remains of another floor of basalt slabs, but from pre-Hispanic times, were detected. Given its characteristics, the specialists deduced that it was part of an open space in the former Palace of Axayácatl, probably a courtyard.
    Barrera and García emphasize that the vestiges of the nascent Viceregal era correspond to reused materials from the Old Houses of Axayácatl. These premises, like so many other structures of the Sacred Precinct of Tenochtitlan, were destroyed by the Spanish and their indigenous allies, almost to their foundations.
    As an example of this, embedded in the façade of the southeast interior corner of the colonial room, two pre-Hispanic dressed stones carved with sculptures in high relief were found. They depicted a feathered serpent (Quetzalcóatl) and a headdress of feathers, and must have belonged to a panel of the Palace of Axayácatl. Also, another Mexica sculpture with the glyph that symbolizes the  tianquiztli or market was recovered, in its place as part of a shaft.
    Some of the diagnostic materials, which allow the preliminary dating of these findings, are the remains of pre-Hispanic and colonial pottery, whose types (styles) belong to the aforementioned historical periods: Aztec III: Orange Monochrome, Black-on-Orange and Texcoco Burnished Earthenware; as well as glazed earthenware and Spanish and New Spain majolica.
        Raúl Barrera, also a researcher at the INAH Directorate of Salvage Archaeology, reflects on the fact that some material evidence speaks volumes, by its very paucity. Such is the case for the recent discoveries in the National Monte de Piedad, which “speak” of the destruction that the main buildings of Tenochtitlan were subjected to, both for symbolic and practical purposes.
    Despite the modest nature of the remains, those pre-Hispanic floors were the same ones that the Spanish invaders and their allies tread upon during their arrival to Tenochtitlan, on November 8, 1519. Moctezuma II allowed them to stay in the old houses that had belonged to his father, and soon after they turned them into their barracks.
    While in that palace, numerous events took place. For example, they remodeled a room to celebrate mass, and right there, they also held various rulers captive, starting with their distinguished host: Moctezuma Xocoyotzin; as well as Cuitláhuac, lord of Iztapalapa; Cacamatzin,  tlatoani of Texcoco; and Itzcuauhtzin, lord of Tlatelolco, among others.
    Following on the massacre during the festival of Tóxcatl that took place at the Templo Mayor (“Great Temple”) on May 22 of 1520, by the order of Pedro de Alvarado, the Spanish withdrew and returned to the Palace of Axayácatl. That incident unleashed the anger of the Mexica and, at the end of June of that year, they besieged the invaders in that building. Hernán Cortés wanted to contain their fury through Moctezuma, who tried to placate them from the roof of the palace. But the efforts were in vain, as his brother Cuitláhuac had already been ordained the new ruler.
    On the night of June 30, 1520, the Spanish fled to Tlaxcala by the great causeway of Tlacopan (today Tacuba), leaving behind the Old Houses of Axayácatl.
        After the fall of Tenochtitlan, the surviving Mexicas were forced to destroy, with their own hands, their temples and palaces and build the new city using the same materials. The Old Houses of Axayácatl would gave way to the Houses of Hernán de Cortés, which were also the temporary headquarters of the first Cabildo of New Spain, around 1525, and of the Marquessate of the Valley of Oaxaca, a noble title carrying the responsibility for creating a government, granted in 1529 for his services to the Spanish Crown.
    Upon the conqueror's death in 1547, his descendants retained ownership. His son Martín Cortés Zúñiga inherited the marquessate, but in 1566 he was involved in a conspiracy that sought to seize the government of New Spain, so he was expelled from the Viceroyalty. The houses and the extensive land remained in the hands of a legal representative and, already in poor condition, were sold to the Sacro Monte de Piedad in 1836.
title: 发现Axayácatl宫殿和Cortés建造的房屋的遗迹
title_en: Remains of the Palace of Axayácatl and a house built by Cortés are discovered

它们感觉很舒服，它们符合我们的轮廓。别管那些洞和旧污渍了🤢。
X11就像旧内衣。它已经存在了30年了……。然而，我们仍然在使用它，尽管它的功能差距和不可靠的怪癖。
它运行Linux，但没有任何遗留的X11代码。因为它针对Wayland出色的移动体验进行了优化。
但是新内衣让人感觉不舒服。因此，今天我们将学习Wayland，并了解应用程序是如何使用Wayland构建的。
希望有一天，随着我们抛弃旧内衣，我们将转向更新、更简单的应用程序框架(如LVGL和Ffltter)：X11、SDL、GTK、Qt等。
X11服务控制Linux应用程序的呈现(以及键盘和鼠标输入)，如下所示……。
在顶部，我们有在Linux机器上运行的Linux程序：终端、编辑器、Web浏览器。
每个程序呈现其图形显示并将原始图形传输到X11服务(通过本地TCP套接字)。
窗口管理器/合成器由桌面环境提供：Xfce、KDE、Gnome、...。
窗口管理器/合成器将渲染的图形包装到显示窗口中，并使用滚动条、标题栏和最小化/最大化/关闭按钮来装饰它们。
然后，窗口管理器/合成器根据它们的屏幕坐标将显示窗口绘制到屏幕缓冲区中。
屏幕缓冲区通过与Linux显示驱动程序对话的X11服务呈现到我们的屏幕上。
任何键盘和鼠标输入都由X11服务捕获，并转发给程序。
这就是我是如何使用(虐待？)。UIUC系统研究小组的X11R4早在1990年(30年前！)。
我们是否需要用标题栏和最小化/最大化/关闭按钮来装饰PinePhone窗口？
以下是Wayland Compositor如何使用Ubuntu Touch控制PinePhone上的应用程序和触摸屏输入…
在顶部，我们的手机上运行着以下应用程序：终端、编辑器、网络浏览器。
当应用程序启动时，它会向Wayland Compositor查询可用的图形显示界面。(他们通过LINUX套接字文件交谈：/RUN/USER/32011/WAYLAND-0)。
APP调用EGL接口将OpenGL图形直接渲染到Linux显示驱动程序。
Linux显示驱动将OpenGL渲染命令转发给GPU以更新屏幕。
任何触摸屏输入都会被Wayland Compositor捕获，并转发到活动的应用程序。
是!。我谎称韦兰是新内衣。韦兰德并不是真的那么新！
“韦兰德”于2008年首次上映(11年前)……。然而，它是围绕OpenGL和GPU设计的，同样的技术也为我们今天美丽的游戏提供了动力。(网站也是如此)。
请继续阅读，了解如何在PinePhone上使用Wayland和Ubuntu Touch渲染我们自己的OpenGL图形。

OpenGL ES针对嵌入式设备进行了优化。如今，许多手机和游戏机游戏都在使用它。

还记得我们如何通过使用CreateSimpleTexture2D()创建OpenGL纹理来渲染简单的2像素x 2像素位图吗？
让我们将位图扩展到覆盖整个PinePhone屏幕：720像素乘以1398像素。
/PinePhone屏幕分辨率，在LV_conf.h#定义LV_HOR_RES_MAX(720)#定义LV_VER_RES_MAX(1398)#定义LV_SCALE_RES 1/屏幕缓冲区#定义Bytes_Per_Pixel 3GLubyte像素[LV_HOR_RES_MAX*LV_VER_RES_MAX*Bytes_Per_Pixel]；/为屏幕缓冲区GLuint CreateTexerGLuint创建OpenGL纹理。GlTexImage2D(GL_Texture_2D，0，//级别GL_RGB，LV_HOR_RES_Max，//宽度LV_VER_RES_Max，//高度0，//格式GL_RGB，GL_UNSIGNED_BYTE，像素)；glTexParameteri(GL_Texture_2D，GL_Texture_Min_Filter，GL_LINEAR)；glTexParameteri(GL_Texture_2D，GL_Texture_MAG_Filter，GL_LINEAR)；glTexParameteri(GL_Texture_2D，GL_Texture_MAG_Filter，GL_LINEAR)。GlTexParameteri(GL_Texture_2D，GL_Texture_WRAP_T，GL_CLAMP_TO_EDGE)；return texId；}。
像素是屏幕缓冲区，它将包含呈现的UI控件(如按钮)的像素。
/设置屏幕缓冲区中像素的颜色void put_px(uint16_t x，uint16_t y，uint8_tr，uint8_tg，uint8_t b，uint8_t a){assert(x&gt；=0)；assert(x&lt；lv_HOR_RES_Max)；assert(y&gt；=0)；assert(y&lt；lv_ver_res。像素[i++]=r；//红色像素[i++]=g；//绿色像素[i++]=b；//蓝色}。
/渲染OpenGL ES2 Displaystatic void Render_Display(){//此部分是新的...。//初始化LVGL display lv_init()；lv_port_disp_init()；//创建LVGL控件：Button和Label Render_widgets()；//渲染LVGL控件LV_TASK_HANDLER()；//此部分与之前相同...。//创建纹理上下文Static ESContext esContext；esInitContext(&amp；esContext)；esContext.width=width；esContext.Height=Height；//绘制纹理Init(&amp；esContext)；Draw(&amp；esContext)；//立即渲染glFlush()；}。
Render_widgets()：调用LVGL库以创建两个UI控件：按钮和标签。
现在，让我们调整LVGL库以将UI控件呈现到我们的屏幕缓冲区像素中
根据LVGL移植文档，我们需要编写一个刷新回调函数disp_flush()，LVGL将调用该函数将UI控件渲染到屏幕缓冲区。
//将内部缓冲区的内容刷新到显示器上的特定区域//您可以在后台使用DMA或任何硬件加速来完成此操作，但//&#39；lv_disp_flush_ady()&#39；必须在完成时调用。static void disp_flush(lv_disp_drv_t*disp_drv，const lv_area_。

尽管五角大楼发表声明称，它解散了一个曾经秘密开展的调查不明飞行物的项目，但这项工作仍在进行中--改名后藏在海军情报办公室(Office Of Naval Intelligence)内，那里的官员继续研究军事飞行员与身份不明的飞行器之间令人费解的遭遇。
五角大楼官员不会讨论该项目，该项目不属于机密，但涉及机密事务。然而，它出现在上个月参议院委员会的一份报告中，该报告概述了来年国家情报机构的支出。报告称，这个名为“不明空中现象特遣部队”的项目是为了“标准化收集和报告”目击不明航行器的行为，并将至少每六个月向公众报告部分调查结果。
虽然参与这一行动的退休官员--包括前参议院多数党领袖哈里·里德(Harry Reid)--希望该项目能寻找来自其他世界的车辆的证据，但它的主要关注点是发现另一个国家，特别是任何潜在的对手，是否正在使用可能威胁美国的突破性航空技术。
参议院情报特别委员会(Senate Select Committee On Intelligence)代理主席、佛罗里达州共和党参议员马尔科·卢比奥(Marco Rubio)本月告诉哥伦比亚广播公司(CBS)在迈阿密的一个分支机构，他主要担心有关美国军事基地上空有不明飞机的报道--找出谁应该对此负责符合政府的利益。
他担心中国、俄罗斯或其他一些对手已经取得了“一些技术飞跃”，“允许他们进行这种活动”。
卢比奥说，美国基地上空的一些身份不明的飞行器可能展示了美国军火库以外的技术。但他也指出：“也许对此有一个完全的、某种意义上的、令人厌烦的解释。但我们需要找出答案。“。
2017年，《纽约时报》披露了一个前身单位的存在，名为高级航空航天威胁识别计划(Advanced AerSpace Threat Identify Program)。国防部官员当时表示，该部队及其2200万美元的资金在2012年后已经失效。
然而，参与该项目的人士表示，该项目在2017年及以后仍在运作，这一声明后来得到了国防部的证实。
该项目始于2007年，隶属于国防情报局(Defense Intelligence Agency)，然后被置于负责情报事务的国防部副部长办公室内，国防部仍负责监督该项目。但它与情报界的协调将由海军情报办公室进行，正如参议院预算法案中所描述的那样。在那些年里，该计划从未失效，但关于2017年后的行动几乎没有披露。
五角大楼项目的前主任路易斯·伊利桑多(Luis Elizabeth Ondo)证实，新的特遣部队是从先进的航空航天项目演变而来的。伊利桑多曾是一名军事情报官员，在该项目工作了10年后于2017年10月辞职。
“它再也不用躲在阴影里了，”伊利桑多说。“它将有一个新的透明度。”
伊丽莎白多是少数拥有安全许可的前政府官员和科学家之一，他们在没有出示实物证据的情况下表示，他们相信来源不明的物体已经坠落在地球上，带着被带回进行研究的材料。
根据对项目参与者的采访和非机密简报文件，十多年来，五角大楼的项目一直在为国会委员会、航空航天公司高管和其他政府官员进行机密简报。
在某些情况下，已经找到了对以前无法解释的事件的世俗解释。天体物理学家说，即使缺乏可信的地球解释，也不会使外星解释成为最有可能的解释。
来自内华达州的前民主党参议员里德(Reid)在担任多数党领袖时曾推动为早先的UFO项目提供资金。他说，他认为来自其他世界的车辆也发生过撞车事故，几十年来，人们一直在秘密研究找回的材料，通常是由政府合同下的航空航天公司进行研究。
里德在接受采访时说：“在调查这件事之后，我得出结论，有报道--有些是实质性的，有些不是很实质性的--有政府和私营部门掌握的实际材料。”
目前还没有公开生产坠机文物供独立核实。一些被找回的物体，如不寻常的金属碎片，后来从实验室研究中被确认为人造的。
天体物理学家埃里克·W·戴维斯(Eric W.Davis)自2007年以来一直担任五角大楼不明飞行物项目的分包商和顾问，他说，在某些情况下，对这些材料的检查到目前为止未能确定它们的来源，并导致他得出结论，“我们自己做不到。”
对讨论机密项目的限制-以及简报的非机密幻灯片中引用的信息的模棱两可-让研究过UFO的官员能够在没有提出任何确凿证据的情况下发表自己的观点。
获取最新的冠状病毒大流行和其他新闻，因为它发生在我们的免费突发新闻电子邮件警报。
戴维斯现在为国防承包商航空航天公司工作，他说，最近在3月份，他向国防部的一个机构做了一次机密简报，内容是从“非地球制造的外星飞行器”中回收。
戴维斯说，他还于2019年10月21日向参议院军事委员会的工作人员以及两天后的参议院情报委员会成员通报了找回不明物体的机密简报。
公众对UFO话题的迷恋吸引了唐纳德·特朗普总统，他告诉他的儿子小唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump Jr.)。在6月份的一次采访中，他知道关于罗斯韦尔的“非常有趣”的事情-新墨西哥州的一个城市，是关于UFO存在的猜测的中心。当被问及是否会解密有关罗斯威尔的任何信息时，总统表示异议。“我得考虑一下这个问题，”他说。
里德说，无论哪种方式，都应该更多地公开，以澄清哪些是已知的，哪些是未知的。他说：“关于发现物理材料或找回的飞行器的信息公布是极其重要的。”
note_en:
Despite Pentagon statements that it disbanded a once-covert program to investigate unidentified flying objects, the effort remains underway — renamed and tucked inside the Office of Naval Intelligence, where officials continue to study mystifying encounters between military pilots and unidentified aerial vehicles.
  Pentagon officials will not discuss the program, which is not classified but deals with classified matters. Yet it appeared last month in a Senate committee report outlining spending on the nation’s intelligence agencies for the coming year. The report said the program, the Unidentified Aerial Phenomenon Task Force, was “to standardize collection and reporting” on sightings of unexplained aerial vehicles, and was to report at least some of its findings to the public every six months.
    While retired officials involved with the effort — including Harry Reid, the former Senate majority leader — hope the program will seek evidence of vehicles from other worlds, its main focus is on discovering whether another nation, especially any potential adversary, is using breakout aviation technology that could threaten the United States.
  Sen. Marco Rubio, R-Fla., who is the acting chairman of the Senate Select Committee on Intelligence, told a CBS affiliate in Miami this month that he was primarily concerned about reports of unidentified aircraft over U.S. military bases — and that it was in the government’s interest to find out who was responsible.
      He expressed concerns that China or Russia or some other adversary had made “some technological leap” that “allows them to conduct this sort of activity.”
  Rubio said some of the unidentified aerial vehicles over U.S. bases possibly exhibited technologies not in the U.S. arsenal. But he also noted: “Maybe there is a completely, sort of, boring explanation for it. But we need to find out.”
      In 2017, The New York Times disclosed the existence of a predecessor unit, called the Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program. Defense Department officials said at the time that the unit and its $22 million in funding had lapsed after 2012.
  People working with the program, however, said it was still in operation in 2017 and beyond, statements later confirmed by the Defense Department.
    The program was begun in 2007 under the Defense Intelligence Agency and was then placed within the office of the undersecretary of defense for intelligence, which remains responsible for its oversight. But its coordination with the intelligence community will be carried out by the Office of Naval Intelligence, as described in the Senate budget bill. The program never lapsed in those years, but little was disclosed about the post-2017 operations.
  The Pentagon program’s previous director, Luis Elizondo, a former military intelligence official who resigned in October 2017 after 10 years with the program, confirmed that the new task force evolved from the advanced aerospace program.
  “It no longer has to hide in the shadows,” Elizondo said. “It will have a new transparency.”
  Elizondo is among a small group of former government officials and scientists with security clearances who, without presenting physical proof, say they are convinced that objects of undetermined origin have crashed on Earth with materials retrieved for study.
    For more than a decade, the Pentagon program has been conducting classified briefings for congressional committees, aerospace company executives and other government officials, according to interviews with program participants and unclassified briefing documents.
  In some cases, earthly explanations have been found for previously unexplained incidents. Even lacking a plausible terrestrial explanation does not make an extraterrestrial one the most likely, astrophysicists say.
  Reid, the former Democratic senator from Nevada who pushed for funding the earlier UFO program when he was the majority leader, said he believed that crashes of vehicles from other worlds had occurred and that retrieved materials had been studied secretly for decades, often by aerospace companies under government contracts.
  “After looking into this, I came to the conclusion that there were reports — some were substantive, some not so substantive — that there were actual materials that the government and the private sector had in their possession,” Reid said in an interview.
    No crash artifacts have been publicly produced for independent verification. Some retrieved objects, such as unusual metallic fragments, were later identified from laboratory studies as man-made.
  Eric W. Davis, an astrophysicist who worked as a subcontractor and then a consultant for the Pentagon UFO program since 2007, said that, in some cases, examination of the materials had so far failed to determine their source and led him to conclude, “We couldn’t make it ourselves.”
  The constraints on discussing classified programs — and the ambiguity of information cited in unclassified slides from the briefings — have put officials who have studied UFOs in the position of stating their views without presenting any hard evidence.
  Get updates on the coronavirus pandemic and other news as it happens with our free breaking news email alerts.
  Davis, who now works for Aerospace Corp., a defense contractor, said he gave a classified briefing to a Defense Department agency as recently as March about retrievals from “off-world vehicles not made on this earth.”
  Davis said he also gave classified briefings on retrievals of unexplained objects to staff members of the Senate Armed Services Committee on Oct. 21, 2019, and to members of the Senate Intelligence Committee two days later.
    Public fascination with the topic of UFOs has drawn in President Donald Trump, who told his son Donald Trump Jr. in a June interview that he knew “very interesting” things about Roswell — a city in New Mexico that is central to speculation about the existence of UFOs. The president demurred when asked if he would declassify any information on Roswell. “I’ll have to think about that one,” he said.
  Either way, Reid said, more should be made public to clarify what is known and what is not. “It is extremely important that information about the discovery of physical materials or retrieved craft come out,” he said.

(7月25日2020/12：38 AM)Neşe Devenot，David Nickles和Brian Normand对这篇报道也有贡献。
2020年7月24日，在美利坚合众国[原文如此]诉威廉·伦纳德·皮卡德一案中，美国堪萨斯州地区法院提交了一份备忘录和命令。这份备忘录和命令声明皮卡德的恩恤释放动议获得批准。
这一决定是在皮卡德因密谋制造、分销和分发LSD而入狱20年后做出的。皮卡德和他的伴侣克莱德·阿普森(Clyde ApPerson)于2000年在堪萨斯州各地转移据称是他们的LSD实验室时被捕，并于2003年被判有罪。皮卡德被判处两次无期徒刑，阿普森-目前仍被监禁-被判处30年监禁，不得假释。
在对DEA负责的特别探员威廉·J·伦顿(William J.Renton，Jr.)宣判时。他说：“这些起诉和定罪的重要性的证据在于，在他们被捕后的两年里，迷幻药在美国的供应减少了95%。”在2008年世界迷幻论坛上，作家乔恩·汉纳(Jon Hanna)代表皮卡德提出了质疑DEA的说法。
在狱中，皮卡德创作并出版了被广泛认为是迷幻文学杰作的“天堂玫瑰：论秘密与圣礼”(The Rose of Paracelsus：On Secrets and Sacraments)。
皮卡德的减刑动议是考虑到他的年龄(74岁)，他日益恶化的身体状况(列出的疾病有慢性肾脏疾病、高血压、贫血、甲状腺功能减退、白内障、玻璃体后脱离、前列腺增生、维生素D缺乏和糖尿病前期)，他面临的新冠肺炎病毒的风险，以及他在撰写芬太尼疫情方面所发挥的积极作用。
根据备忘录和命令，“法院认为减刑符合第3553(A)条规定的量刑因素，”备忘录和命令接着指出：
“皮卡德的罪行很严重，但在监狱里度过了20年后，他受到了严厉的惩罚。政府正确地指出皮卡德有毒品前科，但不质疑被告的论点(Dkt。849，31-32)，第一步法案对毒品犯罪判刑的改变将极大地改变对他既往罪行的待遇[原文如此]。国会现在改变了对待此类罪行的方式，这一事实与根据第3553(A)条确定被告是否面临处境相似的被告之间的差异有关。“。注：§3553(A)是美国判刑的代码。
备忘录和命令承认皮卡德的断言--74岁的他不会对社会构成任何重大风险，并在康复方面做出了大量努力，并指出政府原告没有对这些断言提出质疑。法院的结论是，服刑20年足以达到丧失工作能力、威慑、报复和康复的目标。
该决定还关注了监狱管理局为减少新冠肺炎在其监狱内的传播所做的努力：
“法院的结论是，皮卡德如果感染新冠肺炎，比普通囚犯患重病的风险更大，而且他还面临一些接触病毒的风险。不幸的是，正如政府回应中记录的那样，对英国防务部门限制病毒传播努力的乐观描述已经被事件所取代。在全国范围内，国际收支已经完成了33684次检测，9936次检测呈阳性。皮卡德被关押在亚利桑那州的FCI Tucson，那里已经有11名囚犯和工作人员被诊断出感染了病毒。目前还没有囚犯或工作人员死于这种病毒。监狱内部的社交距离是很难的。“。(已添加强调)。
备忘录中对国际防务部门无力保护囚犯免受流行病威胁的承认，突显了此时此刻废除死刑的重要性。虽然皮卡德很快就会获释，但现实是，囚犯中的许多人是黑人、布朗人和穷人，他们将继续面临监禁的恐怖，现在这种流行病放大了这种恐惧。随着新冠肺炎在美国的监狱中横冲直撞，许多禁毒战争中的囚犯现在可能因轻微的罪行而面临死刑。
备忘录和命令写道：“第3553(A)条的因素支持将被告的刑期减少到服刑时间，随后是五年的监督释放。”
纽约企业家迈克·杜普勒(Mike Dupler)是皮卡德的好朋友，他在一封电子邮件中告诉Psymposa，他和一小群“帮手”以及世界各地的无数支持者一直在为皮卡德要求“减刑和体恤释放”的请愿书做力所能及的事情。他说，杜普勒之所以参与帮助皮卡德和他的律师，是因为他无法忍受生活在一个非暴力毒品犯罪可能导致终身监禁的国家。
杜普勒说：“如此严重的不公正必须以某种方式得到解决，今天法官这样做了。”“美国东部时间今天下午1点左右，当我读到法官对莱纳德案件的裁决时，我被告知了这一决定。说我们有多幸福就有多幸福，说得委婉些。我与伦纳德的律师交换了电子邮件，她告诉我，她今天哭得很痛快--幸福的泪水。20年前，她和她的前法律合伙人(现已过世)也曾代表伦纳德出庭受审。“。
“我迫不及待地想在监狱环境之外见到他，”杜普勒说。
(2020-07-24/6：32 PM)医学博士朱莉·霍兰德(@BellevueDoc)报道，LSD化学家、长期毒品战争囚犯威廉·伦纳德·皮卡德(William Leonard Pickard)即将出狱。皮卡德自2000年以来一直被关押，并被判处两次终身监禁，不得假释。
对于那些认识他，关心他的困境，或者像我一样代表他写信的人，威廉·伦纳德·皮卡德将被释放，充满同情心。

(July 25, 2020 / 12:38AM)    Neşe Devenot  ,    David Nickles  , and Brian Normand contributed to this story.
 On July 24, 2020, a Memorandum and Order was filed in the United States District Court of Kansas, in the case of   United States of American [sic] vs. William Leonard Pickard . This Memorandum and Order states that Pickard’s motion for compassionate release is granted.
 The decision comes after Pickard spent 20 years in prison for conspiracy to manufacture, distribute, and dispense LSD. Pickard and his partner, Clyde Apperson, were arrested in 2000 while moving what was alleged to be their LSD laboratory across Kansas, and they were convicted in 2003. Pickard was given two life sentences and Apperson—who is still incarcerated—was sentenced to 30 years of imprisonment without the possibility of parole.
 At his sentencing DEA Special Agent in Charge William J. Renton, Jr.   stated , “The proof of the significance of these prosecutions and convictions lies in the fact that LSD availability in the United States was reduced by 95% in the two years following their arrest.” At the 2008 World Psychedelic Forum, writer Jon Hanna—  presenting on behalf  of Pickard—questioned the DEA’s claims.
 While in prison, Pickard wrote and published   The Rose of Paracelsus: On Secrets and Sacraments , which is widely regarded as a masterpiece of psychedelic literature.
   Pickard’s motion for a reduction of sentence was made in light of his age (  74 ), his deteriorating medical condition (  listed as chronic kidney disease, hypertension, anemia, hypothyroidism, cataracts, posterior vitreous detachment, prostatic hyperplasia, vitamin D deficiency and pre-diabetes ), the risk he faces from the COVID-19 virus, and the positive role he has played in writing about the fentanyl epidemic.
 “ The court finds that a reduction in sentence is consistent with the sentencing factors set forth in § 3553(a),” according to the Memorandum and Order, which goes on to state:
 “Pickard’s offenses were serious, but having spent two decades in prison he has been seriously punished. The government correctly points out that Pickard has prior drug convictions, but does not challenge the defendant’s argument (Dkt. 849, at 31-32) that the First Step Act’s change to sentences for drug crimes would substantially changed [sic] the treatment of his prior offenses. The fact that Congress has changed how such offenses are treated now is relevant to the determination whether a defendant faces disparities among similarly-situated defendants under § 3553(a).” Note: § 3553(a) is the US code for Imposition of a Sentence.
  The Memorandum and Order acknowledges Pickard’s  assertions that—at 74 years of age—he does not pose any significant risk to society and has engaged in substantial efforts at rehabilitation, and  notes that the government plaintiff has not challenged these assertions . The court concluded that the 20 years served are sufficient for meeting the goals of incapacitation, deterrence, retribution, and rehabilitation.
 The decision also focuses on the Bureau of Prison’s (BOP’s) efforts to reduce the spread of COVID-19 within its prisons:
 “The court concludes that Pickard is at greater risk than the average inmate of suffering a severe illness should he contract COVID-19, and further that he faces some risk of exposure to the virus.  Unfortunately, the optimistic portrayal of BOP efforts to limit the spread of the virus, as documented in the government’s Response, has been overtaken by events.  Nationwide, the BOP has completed 33,684 tests, with 9,936 showing a positive result. Pickard is incarcerated at FCI Tucson, Arizona, where 11 inmates and staff members have been diagnosed with the virus. No inmates or staff have died of the virus. Social distancing within the prison is difficult.” (emphasis added)
 This acknowledgement of the BOP’s inability to protect inmates from the threats of pandemic in the Memorandum underscores the importance of abolition in this moment. While Pickard will soon be free, the reality is that prisoners, many of them Black, Brown, and poor people will continue to face the horrors of incarceration, now magnified by the pandemic. With COVID-19 running rampant through America’s carceral facilities, many prisoners of the war on [some people who use certain] drugs are now potentially facing death sentences for minor offenses.
 “The § 3553(a) factors support reducing [the] defendant’s sentence to time served, to be followed by a five-year term of supervised release,” the Memorandum and Order reads.
 Mike Dupler—a New York City-based entrepreneur and a good friend of Pickard—told Psymposia in an email that he, along with a small group of “helpers” and countless supporters around the world, have been doing what they can for Pickard’s petition for “reduction in sentence and compassionate release.” Dupler became involved in helping Pickard and his attorney because he could not bear the thought of living in a country where a non-violent drug offense could result in a life sentence due to “mandatory sentencing,” he said.
 “An injustice of this magnitude had to be addressed somehow and today the Judge did so,” Dupler said. “I was informed about the judge’s decision in Leonard’s case when I read his order this afternoon around 1PM EST. To say we are happy as happy can be is to put it mildly. I exchanged emails with Leonard’s attorney and she told me that she had a good cry today—tears of happiness. She and her former law partner (now deceased) had also represented Leonard at his trial 20 years ago.”
 “I cannot wait to see him outside of the prison environment,” Dupler said.
   (July 24, 2020 / 6:32PM) Julie Holland, M.D. ( @BellevueDoc) is reporting that LSD-chemist and longtime drug war prisoner, William Leonard Pickard, is being released from prison. Pickard has been locked down since 2000 and was slated to serve two life sentences without parole.
 For those of you who know him, or care about his plight, or wrote letters on his behalf as I did, William Leonard Pickard is being released, compassionately.

  This means we require support from readers like you - the grass roots - so we can remain independent and keep digging into the biggest issues  with no strings attached. You can make a huge impact by supporting us for as little as $2 a month.

昨天，微软在欧洲发布了一份竞争投诉，“跳到主要内容松弛”让微软大吃一惊。经过几个月的争辩，认为微软团队不是Slake的真正竞争对手，更类似于Zoom，Slake终于承认了一直以来都很清楚的一件事：微软团队是竞争对手，而Slake发现很难与微软竞争。这并不令人惊讶，但如果Slake发现很难与微软竞争，那么一旦谷歌最终采取行动，它将面临更令人头疼的问题。在摸索了多年的通信应用程序之后，有初步迹象表明，谷歌现在已经准备好与Slake、微软团队和Zoom展开竞争。
谷歌的企业行动对Slake的欧盟反垄断竞标有着巨大的影响--以及该公司在这之后的未来。Sack似乎将面临两家巨头科技公司的挑战，它们利用自己的主导产品在工作场所通信业务中分一大杯羹。如果Slake设法说服欧盟对微软的捆绑行为采取行动，它仍然面临着谷歌以类似方式捆绑自己的应用和服务的迫在眉睫的威胁。对于反垄断斗士来说，G Suite表明捆绑问题比微软大得多。
Sack昨天发布的竞争投诉完全是针对微软的，重点是该公司的Office365订阅捆绑了多个团队。“我们要求的是将团队从Office套件中分离出来，并以公平的商业价格单独出售，这样它就可以在优点上与我们的产品竞争，”Slake的法务主管大卫·谢尔哈斯(David Schellhase)昨日在接受记者电话采访时解释道。“事情真的就这么简单明了。”
几十年来，微软一直在其Office套件中捆绑了各种生产力应用，早在2016年推出时，它就选择了向Office 365客户免费捆绑团队。这种捆绑，加上紧密的Office集成，使得Slake很难说服那些已经在为Office付费的企业支付额外的费用来获得Slake。
但谷歌似乎准备复制这一策略。包括Gmail老用户在内的G Suite在今年早些时候已超过20亿活跃用户，G Suite的新老板Javier Soltero当时表示，“改变人们的工作方式是我们独一无二的定位.”
索尔特罗在微软工作了四年后，最近来到谷歌，他最初是在软件巨头收购ACompli时加入这家公司的，ACompli后来成为iOS Outlook。他已经展示了自己在发现趋势和填补空白方面的专长，这些应用和服务足够好，足以让微软收购。如果他能在谷歌重复这一点，那么Slake就有另一个巨大的竞争对手准备捆绑和利用其广受欢迎的通信和生产力应用程序。
谷歌已经显示出正在追赶Zoom、微软团队和Slake的迹象。谷歌在今年早些时候免费提供Meet，试图与Zoom的突然流行竞争，并已开始将这款视频会议应用深度集成到Gmail和Google Calendar中。迈向真正的松弛和团队竞争的下一步是谷歌将谷歌聊天、聊天室和会议整合到Gmail中的早期工作。这要到今年晚些时候才能实现，但这显然是谷歌的一大优先事项。
如果谷歌真的能在这里执行，并提供一个更连贯的沟通平台，将电子邮件、聊天和视频通话整合到一个单一的体验中，那么这对Slake和微软团队来说都是一个巨大的威胁。
Sack没有很好地应对谷歌迫在眉睫的威胁，以及为什么谷歌的捆绑方式没有微软的威胁那么大。“谷歌和微软是不同的，”谢尔哈斯在回答有关为什么微软与团队的合作与谷歌最近的做法不同的问题时说。“微软在Office生产力套件和所有辅助软件方面占据主导地位。没有法律禁止占据主导地位，但有关于拥有主导市场份额的公司必须如何行为的法律。有一件事他们不能做，那就是把一款新的独立产品与他们已有的主导产品捆绑在一起。“。
如果你看看谷歌和微软的影响力或统治地位之间的原始数据，Office的用户约为12亿，而谷歌称G Suite的用户为20亿。这两个数字之间的关键区别在于，绝大多数使用Office的人将其作为工作许可或订阅的一部分，而谷歌所称的G Suite用户中的绝大多数是大约15亿Gmail用户，他们可能并不都在使用这项服务进行工作。到目前为止，谷歌还没有专注于利用这些免费用户成为企业客户-但一旦启动，它可能会在一夜之间成为主要参与者。
微软凭借Office在职场占据主导地位，但谷歌显然主导着消费者对电子邮件、搜索以及YouTube等服务的使用。谷歌的免费服务也用于工作。在教育领域尤其如此，G Suite和Chromebook继续占据着全美的教室。谷歌将Meet免费与Gmail捆绑和集成的能力仍然应该引起Slake的担忧，即使该公司还不愿意承认这一点，也不愿打这场仗。
目前还不清楚欧盟委员会是否会正式调查斯拉克的申诉。在做出决定之前，我们可能会有几个月的不确定性，对于微软、谷歌、Slake、Zoom和其他许多为企业和学生沟通方式而战的公司来说，这是几个月前的关键。
微软首席执行官萨蒂亚·纳德拉(Satya Nadella)在4月份表示：“我们在两个月内就看到了两年的数字化转型。”在大流行期间，企业一直蜂拥至微软团队(Microsoft Teams)和Zoom等服务。虽然微软团队在一年前以1300万日用户超过了Slake的使用量，但这还不足以引发欧盟的投诉。很明显，在这场流行病中，企业被迫加速的数字化转型将更多的人推向了微软团队，而不是Slake。
在大流行开始时，微软团队的使用率在一周内飙升了近40%，从3200万增加到4400万。这一转变也没有放缓，微软早在4月份就透露，团队现在的日活跃用户为7500万。Sack表示，由于对远程工作的需求增加，它已经打破了用户记录，但该公司表示，到目前为止，只有1250万并发Slack用户。这一数字也不同于Slake此前在10月份披露的1200万日活跃用户。
微软已经对Slake的欧盟投诉做出了回应，该公司利用这个机会强调了它认为Slake错过的一个领域：视频会议。微软表示，虽然Slake确实支持视频会议，但“有了新冠肺炎，市场接纳的团队数量达到了创纪录的水平，而Slake则因为没有视频会议而蒙受了损失。”Sack的视频会议远远逊于团队，这也是Slake与亚马逊合作转向Chime进行语音和视频通话的主要原因。
Sack错过了可靠的视频通话和视频会议，突显了微软团队和Slake之间的主要区别之一。微软利用了对Lync和Skype的投资，并将它们组合成团队和聊天工具，而Slake则出色地将IRC应用于工作场所，并雄心勃勃地希望真正消除商业电子邮件。
Slake和团队之间的差异使两者都可以争夺不同的客户，特别是在微软迎合Office人群和Slake为G Suite、Zoom和其他工具的组合提供服务的情况下。不过，谷歌正在隐约可见。Google Meet与Gmail Hits的更紧密集成是Slake的弱点，如果Google能够产生一个令人信服的Slake竞争对手，那么Slake将面临比单独微软大得多的问题。
科技一项新的Oculus Quest似乎已经从各个角度泄露出去，可能很快就会推出。
苹果Mac可能会获得面部ID，MacOS Big Sur中的代码可能会揭示这一点。
谷歌一些Nest恒温器不能再连接到互联网，所以谷歌正在更换它们。
